question: How many of your employees have access to production systems? What insider threat controls do you have?
title: Production Access Control, Insider Threat Mitigation, and Privileged Access Governance
bullets: Quantify and justify production access (named roles/individuals, least privilege, separation of duties)
Describe access model (zero standing access/JIT, break-glass procedures, approval workflows)
Detail authentication and session controls (MFA with hardware tokens, session recording, command logging)
Explain insider threat detection (behavioral analytics, anomaly detection) and auditability (immutable logs, periodic reviews)
suggested_inputs: Privileged Access Management (PAM) policy and role matrix
List of roles with production access and justification (anonymized if needed)
Access logs and audit trail samples (last 90 days)
Insider threat monitoring reports and background check policy

good.text: Production access is limited to a small, defined set of roles/individuals with clear justification. Access follows least privilege and separation of duties, with periodic reviews and removal of unused privileges.
We use zero standing access (Just-In-Time). Break-glass access requires dual authorization, is time-limited, fully logged, and reviewed post-use by security.
All privileged access requires MFA with hardware tokens. Sessions are recorded with command-level logging, and all actions are captured in immutable audit logs.

We implement behavioral analytics and anomaly detection for insider threats. Access is continuously monitored, with alerts for unusual activity and regular audits. Background checks are mandatory for privileged roles.

partial.text: We restrict production access to authorized personnel, but do not provide specific numbers or detailed role-based breakdowns.
MFA is enforced, but hardware tokens, session recording, or detailed command logging may not be consistently applied.
Access reviews are performed periodically, but processes may be manual or inconsistently enforced.
We monitor access logs, but lack advanced behavioral analytics or real-time anomaly detection.

bad.text: All engineers have production access for agility.
Employees sign NDAs to protect data.
We cannot provide details on who has access or how it is controlled.

follow_ups: Provide a list of roles (or anonymized individuals) with production access, their permissions, and justification.
Do you implement Just-In-Time access? Describe the break-glass process and approval workflow.
Can you provide audit logs showing who accessed our data in the last 90 days?
How do you detect insider threats (behavioral analytics, anomaly detection)?
What is your background check process for employees with privileged access?
